name: electron-to-tauri
title: Electron to Tauri Migration
description: A project-specific dev team that migrates an Electron desktop app to Tauri using a side-by-side strategy — reducing installer size ~90%, memory ~85%, and launch time ~85%. Frontend (React/Svelte) is kept; the Node.js backend is rewritten in Rust. 1:1 implementation of the offline-pixel Electron-to-Tauri migration guide.

roles.name: migration-lead
roles.title: Lead Desktop Engineer
roles.owns: 5-phase plan, assessment, target architecture, side-by-side strategy, go/no-go cutover, rollback decision, cost & success-metric tracking

roles.name: tauri-engineer
roles.title: Rust Developer (or frontend engineer learning Rust)
roles.owns: Node.js → Rust rewrite, ipcRenderer.invoke → Tauri commands, fs → std::fs/tokio::fs, native modules → crates, emit/listen, LevelDB → SQLite data migration, Tauri security allowlist

roles.name: qa-engineer
roles.title: QA Engineer
roles.owns: CI on all 3 OS / WebView differences, side-by-side functional parity tests, memory/launch/installer benchmarking, rollback verification, success-metric validation

phases.summary: Set up Tauri project, migrate frontend build pipeline, train team on Rust, build side-by-side launcher.
Migrate settings and about page — prove Tauri works, zero risk.
Migrate file system operations, system tray, notifications — 80% of functionality. Node→Rust rewrite + LevelDB→SQLite data migration.
Migrate remaining features, run side-by-side, remove Electron entirely after 2 months of zero rollbacks.
